rationale: Verus exit 0 on det.rs. Wrapped det.rs in verus! with scaffolding for KeyTrait/VerusClone/Ordering/StrictlyOrderedVec. Locally made the struct use #[verifier::ext_equal] with an open view returning a Seq field, so extensional struct equality reduces to view equality. Proof block does an explicit case split: if all the conjuncted postconditions of remove hold, derive r1==r2 (both equal pre@.index(i)), then post1@ =~= post2@ (both equal pre@.remove(i)), then post1 =~= post2 by struct extensionality. This discharges the implication-style postcondition (P ==> det_remove_equal). Note: the real source has v: Vec<K> with a closed view, so the same proof block may not verify under re-injection because Vec lacks a view-equality axiom and the closed view hides the relationship between StrictlyOrderedVec@ and the v field.
